article_title: Microsoft Agent Framework 1.0 Ships, Retiring Semantic Kernel and AutoGen as It Bets on a Unified Enterprise SDK
summary: Four errors: 'maintenance mode' framing and retirement quote not in source; 75K/9.4K stars and 125 contributors not in sources; specific breaking changes not in InfoQ; versions 1.1.0/1.2.0 with dates and features not in sources.

content_preview.title: Microsoft Agent Framework 1.0 Ships, Retiring Semantic Kernel and AutoGen as It Bets on a Unified Enterprise SDK
content_preview.summary: Microsoft's Agent Framework 1.0 merges Semantic Kernel and AutoGen into a single production-ready SDK for building multi-agent AI systems in .NET and Python.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Microsoft shipped Agent Framework 1.0 on April 3, 2026, ending more than six months of preview releases and officially retiring two of the company's most widely used open-source AI projects — Semantic Kernel and AutoGen — as primary development targets. The 1.0 milestone marks the framework's graduation to stable APIs and a long-term support commitment, positioning it as Microsoft's canonical SDK for building enterprise AI agents in .NET and Python.

The release comes as agent-based...

findings_appended.message: 'Maintenance mode' framing and 'New feature development' quote not in cited source
findings_appended.details: The article states SK and AutoGen 'are now transitioning to maintenance mode — receiving security patches and critical bug fixes, but no significant new features' and quotes 'New feature development is moving to Agent Framework going forward.' The devblogs.microsoft.com/agent-framework announcement does not state either frameworks are being retired or moved to maintenance mode; it offers migration assistance instead. The exact quote does not appear in the source. Remove or properly source the 'maintenance mode' claim and the associated direct quote.

findings_appended.message: Star counts and contributor count not in cited sources
findings_appended.details: The article claims 'combined GitHub repositories for Semantic Kernel and AutoGen had accumulated more than 75,000 stars' and the 'unified repository launched with approximately 9,400 stars and more than 125 contributors at general availability.' Neither figure appears in any of the three cited sources. Remove these statistics or cite a verifiable source (e.g., a direct GitHub repo link with star counts).

findings_appended.message: Specific breaking changes (BaseContextProvider, BaseHistoryProvider, Message constructor) not in InfoQ source
findings_appended.details: The article attributes 'the removal of deprecated BaseContextProvider and BaseHistoryProvider aliases and changes to the Message constructor API' to the InfoQ RC article, but the InfoQ source only generically mentions 'minor breaking changes if needed.' These specific breaking changes are not mentioned in InfoQ or the main announcement. Remove the specific breaking-change names or cite a source that lists them explicitly.

findings_appended.message: Versions 1.1.0 and 1.2.0 with specific dates and features not in sources
findings_appended.details: The article states 'Versions 1.1.0 and 1.2.0, released on April 21 and April 24 respectively, have already followed the initial release, adding Google Gemini integration, OpenTelemetry support for GitHub Copilot agents, and the first Agent Framework-to-A2A bridge support.' None of this — the version numbers, dates, or specific features — appears in any of the three cited sources. Remove or properly source these claims.

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured 892-word News piece. Clear section organization with overview, technical features, protocol interoperability, RC background, unknowns, and future outlook. The 'What We Don't Know' section appropriately addresses language gaps, community response, and incomplete A2A support.
editor_notes.source_verification: devblogs.microsoft.com/agent-framework (main announcement): fetched — April 3 2026 GA date ✓; multi-provider connectors (Azure OpenAI, OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, Amazon Bedrock, Google Gemini, Ollama) ✓; YAML declarative agents ✓; DevUI debugger ✓; maintenance mode for SK/AutoGen NOT confirmed ✗; 'New feature development' quote NOT found ✗; 75K combined stars NOT found ✗; 9.4K stars at GA NOT found ✗; 125 contributors NOT found ✗; IChatClient from Microsoft.Extensions.AI NOT mentioned ✗; 1.1.0/1.2.0 versions NOT mentioned ✗. devblogs.microsoft.com/foundry: fetched — MCP integration ✓; Azure Developer CLI (azd deploy) ✓; sub-100ms startup ('under 100ms') ✓; VNET support ✓; session isolation ✓; A2A protocol ✓; Microsoft 365/Teams integration ✓. InfoQ (February 2026 RC): fetched — RC date February 26 2026 ✓; exact quote 'stable and feature-complete for what is planned in version 1.0' ✓; InfoQ confirms SK/AutoGen succession ✓; BaseContextProvider/BaseHistoryProvider/Message constructor changes NOT mentioned ✗ (only generic 'minor breaking changes').
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Four sourcing errors: (1) SK/AutoGen 'maintenance mode' framing and 'New feature development' quote not in the announcement; (2) star counts (75K combined, 9.4K at GA) and 125 contributors not in any source; (3) specific breaking changes (BaseContextProvider, BaseHistoryProvider, Message constructor) attributed to InfoQ but not in that article; (4) versions 1.1.0/1.2.0 with specific dates (April 21, April 24) and features (Google Gemini, OpenTelemetry, A2A bridge) not in any source. Core technical content (connectors list, YAML agents, DevUI, MCP, Azure CLI deployment, sub-100ms startup, A2A) all verified.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Appropriately measured in the 'What We Don't Know' section. Competitive context (Google Agent Development Kit, OpenAI Agents SDK, LangGraph) is factual.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Machine Herald coverage of Microsoft Agent Framework 1.0. New and timely topic.
concerns: 'Maintenance mode' framing and retirement quote not confirmed in the main announcement
Star counts (75K combined, 9.4K at GA, 125 contributors) not in any cited source
Specific breaking changes (BaseContextProvider, BaseHistoryProvider, Message constructor API) not in InfoQ source
Versions 1.1.0/1.2.0 with April 21/24 dates and specific features not in any cited source
recommendations: Remove or source the 'maintenance mode' claim and associated quote — the announcement only mentions migration assistance, not explicit retirement
Remove star count statistics (75K, 9.4K, 125 contributors) or link directly to GitHub repos for verification
Remove the specific breaking change names (BaseContextProvider, BaseHistoryProvider, Message constructor) or cite a source that lists them explicitly (e.g., changelog or release notes URL)
Remove the 1.1.0/1.2.0 version details or cite a source such as the GitHub releases page or a changelog
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Solid technical article with good structure and verified core content. Four sourcing errors follow the same pattern: specific claims attributed to sources that don't contain them. Fixes are straightforward — remove unverifiable specifics or add proper citations.
